tool_input.summary: Catalog search did not find any datasets explicitly tagged with the region name “Arabia Terra” in title/metadata. The most relevant approach in PDS is to use global Mars imaging/topography and targeted MRO/Mars Express products (CTX/CRISM/HRSC/MOLA, plus MOC context) and then subset by Arabia Terra coordinates in your analysis. Below are the best matching PDS datasets (IDs verified in tool results) to assess whether deposits record a single vs diverse fluvial depositional styles.

candidates.title: VOLUME MROX_0001: MRO CTX EDR ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: CTX ~6 m/pixel context imaging is ideal for mapping Arabia Terra channel networks, fan/delta morphologies, and stratigraphic relationships over broad areas; you can mosaic and classify fluvial styles across the region.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Targeted CRISM RDRs provide mineralogy (e.g., phyllosilicates/sulfates) that helps distinguish fluvial-lacustrine deposits, altered floodplain materials, and possible groundwater-related units—key for testing diversity of depositional systems.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-MULTISPECTRAL-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: CRISM multispectral mapping gives broader spatial coverage than targeted observations and is useful for regional context in Arabia Terra when evaluating whether deposits represent multiple environments.

candidates.title: MGS-M-MOLA-5-MEGDR-L3-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: MOLA gridded topography underpins drainage-basin delineation, paleoslope analysis, and fan/delta gradient comparisons—core metrics for differentiating fluvial styles and depositional settings across Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-3-RDR-V4.0
candidates.reasoning: Mars Express HRSC image products provide regional stereo-capable imaging useful for mapping depositional landforms at intermediate scale and tying CTX/CRISM interpretations into broader geomorphic context.

candidates.title: MEX-M-HRSC-5-REFDR-DTM-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: HRSC DTMs complement MOLA by adding higher-resolution topography in places—helpful for discriminating between, e.g., alluvial-fan vs deltaic/lacustrine morphologies and assessing channel/fan gradients.

candidates.title: VOLUME MGSC_0001: MOC DSDP ARCHIVE
candidates.reasoning: MOC wide-angle/narrow-angle imaging provides additional legacy context and can help with stratigraphic/landform identification where available, supporting multi-dataset geomorphic comparisons in Arabia Terra.

candidates.title: Atmospheric Retrievals for Mars Integrated from MRO-CRISM Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Only clearly relevant PDS4 CRISM-related bundle surfaced in this catalog slice; while not a geology product, it can support improved CRISM surface interpretation by accounting for atmospheric effects (indirectly aiding compositional discrimination of depositional units).

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Do the Arabia Terra deposits record only one fluvial style or a diverse range of depositional systems?
